Mathematics · Cryptography · Blockchain

Mucahit Ozturk

Mathematics graduate researching cryptography, blockchain technologies and zero-knowledge proofs — turning rigorous math into practical, secure systems.

Get in touch GitHub ↗ LinkedIn ↗
About

Profile

I am a Mathematics graduate and a cryptography researcher with a focus on blockchain technologies and secure computing. My work centers on the rigorous mathematical analysis and formal design of cryptographic protocols. Through my research roles at Nethermind and TUBITAK BILGEM, I have gained hands-on experience in implementing cryptographic primitives for large-scale systems, including Digital Turkish Lira and secure custody solutions. I possess strong analytical skills in Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKP) and Post-Quantum Cryptography. I am adept at deconstructing complex algebraic structures to optimize security and performance. I am highly motivated to apply my background in applied mathematics to develop next-generation security paradigms and resilient digital infrastructures.

Experience

Where I've worked

06.2026 – 08.2026

Cryptography Research Collaborator

Nethermind

Research on hash-based SNARKs (Zinc+ framework) under the supervision of Albert Garreta (Nethermind): post-quantum signature arithmetization, soundness analysis, and constraint system design.

07.2024 – 05.2026

Cryptography Scholarship Recipient

TUBITAK BILGEM(The Scientific and Technological Research Council of Turkiye — Informatics and Information Security Research Center) — Blockchain Research Laboratory

Worked part-time as a cryptography research scholarship recipient focused on zero-knowledge proof systems including SNARKs and Bulletproofs. Authored a comprehensive survey on the transition of cryptocurrencies to post-quantum security, and researched Multi-Party Computation (MPC) and custody solutions for banking systems, including digital and post-quantum signature schemes used in blockchain.

02.2024 – 03.2024

Computer Vision Internship

Pi Robotik

Contributed to an Arçelik-sponsored computer vision project — processing real-time high-resolution camera data with Halcon, developing a C# interface, and enabling robotic system integration.

Research & Projects

What I work on

Zero-Knowledge · zk-SNARK

Zinc+

Falcon Signature Aggregation — Arithmetization of Falcon-512 verification in Zinc+ for post-quantum signature aggregation, with focus on norm-bound constraints and soundness. R1CS/CCS Support — Extending Zinc+ with R1CS/CCS support via ideal checks.

Zero-Knowledge · zk-SNARK

PLONK

Study of the PLONK proving system — a universal, updatable SNARK using a single trusted setup, polynomial commitments and permutation arguments to build efficient zero-knowledge proofs.

Zero-Knowledge · Range Proofs

Bulletproofs

Research on Bulletproofs — short, trustless-setup non-interactive zero-knowledge range proofs based on the inner-product argument, widely used for confidential transactions.

Poster (PDF) ↗ Presentation (PDF) ↗
Cryptanalysis · DRP

A Tutorial on Differential Cryptanalysis

Student researcher in the Directed Reading Program, studying Differential Attacks on DES with Hakan Önder Güneyi. Published a research paper and presented to undergraduate students.

Presentation ↗ Report (PDF) ↗
Post-Quantum · Blockchain

Migrating Cryptocurrencies to the Post-Quantum Era

Survey on the transition of cryptocurrencies to post-quantum security — analysing the threat quantum computing poses to current signature schemes and the quantum-resistant alternatives for blockchain systems.

Report (PDF) ↗
Number Theory · ECC

Elliptic Curve Discrete Logarithm Problem

Study of the ECDLP — the hardness assumption underpinning elliptic-curve cryptography — and the algorithms used to attack it. Presented as an academic poster.

Poster (PDF) ↗
AI · Mobile · 2024

AI-Supported Outdoor Stimulator App

Awarded under the Turkish Health Institutes Presidency A1 Project Support Program for Undergraduate Students.

Machine Learning

Urban Sound Classification

Sound classification model built on the UrbanSound8K dataset.

Deep Learning · CNN

Dog Breed Classification

Image classification using Convolutional Neural Networks.

Cryptography Challenge

Can you spot the weakness?

A protocol I designed myself — a Secure Authenticated Message Protocol (Alice → Bob) built from standard primitives. It aims for confidentiality, integrity, authenticity, forward secrecy, replay protection and identity binding. There is one subtle security gap. Can you find what is missing to make it fully secure?

Confidentiality (AEAD) Integrity (GCM Tag) Authenticity (Ed25519) Forward Secrecy (Ephemeral ECDHE) Replay Protection Identity Binding

Alice (Sender)

0 · Long-term keysEd25519 (sk_A, pk_A), X.509 Cert_A
1 · Receive Bob's certVerify X.509, CA signature, revocation/expiry
2 · Ephemeral key (X25519)Generate (e_A, E_A)
3 · Shared secretS = e_A · E_B
4 · TranscriptT = E_A ∥ E_B ∥ Cert_B
5 · Key derivation(K_enc, N) ← HKDF(S, H(T))
6 · MessageM = "This internship suits me."
7 · Identity-binding signatureσ = Sign_skA(M ∥ E_A ∥ E_B)
8 · Authenticated encryption(C, Tag) = AES-256-GCM_Kenc(M ∥ σ, N)
9 · SendCert_A, E_A, C, Tag

Bob (Receiver)

0 · Long-term keysEd25519 (sk_B, pk_B), X.509 Cert_B
1 · Ephemeral key (X25519)Generate (e_B, E_B); send Cert_B, E_B
2 · Shared secretS = e_B · E_A
3 · TranscriptT = E_A ∥ E_B ∥ Cert_B
4 · Key derivation(K_enc, N) ← HKDF(S, H(T))
5 · Integrity checkVerify GCM Tag
6 · DecryptRecover M and σ
7 · Certificate checkVerify Cert_A, extract pk_A
8 · Signature checkVerify σ; ensure binding to E_A, E_B
Bob → Alice : Cert_B, E_B
Alice → Bob : Cert_A, E_A, C, Tag

Your move

The protocol looks complete — but one of its stated security goals is not actually achieved. Think about what each party can really prove about the other, and what is (and is not) authenticated on the wire.

Found it? I'd love to hear your answer — reach out below.
